title: Joby Outlines Dubai Air Taxi Plans Ahead of FAA Certification
text: Joby Aviation has outlined detailed plans for launching its air taxi service in Dubai ahead of completing FAA type certification in the United States. The company has secured an exclusive six-year operating agreement with Dubai's Roads and Transport Authority to operate electric air taxis in the emirate. The Dubai service represents an important milestone because it allows Joby to begin commercial operations before receiving its FAA type certificate. The company is building four vertiports in Dubai: one at Dubai International Airport, one at Dubai Mall, one at Atlantis the Royal resort, and one at American University of Dubai. These facilities will serve as hubs for Joby's air taxi operations throughout the city. The company completed the first point-to-point flight in the UAE on November 9, 2025, demonstrating its readiness for commercial operations. CEO JoeBen Bevirt stated that Joby is making incredible progress and looking ahead to launching commercial passenger service in Dubai in 2026. The aircraft is designed to carry a pilot and four passengers at speeds up to 200 miles per hour, with a journey from Dubai International Airport to Palm Jumeirah expected to take just 10 minutes compared with 45 minutes by car.
